comment.body:
your logic is good, but not entirely correct.
1) yes, the purpose of that call is to create a synthetic exposure against the new collar, so there is no extra synthetic short exposure against their long equity portfolio exposures (in their brochoure they put it down clear, what % they hedge: no more and no less, never)
2) the new collar is set at 2PM... you have 2 hours until market close. If you buy OTM call, your delta decays fast = no delta hedge against the collar.
if you buy ATM, same problem bcs you are exposed to the price swings.
If you buy ITM, you have positive charm, your deltas will approach 1, at expo.

So the answer is a deep ITM 0DTE call

comment.body:
GM, Alma, would you like to tell us the answer about yesterday's question? or confirm if Leonida's answer is correct or not?
1) whats the purpose of the 0DTE call?
2) what happens to its delta during the day?

Answer from Leonida: 
1)Hedging the negative extra synthetic delta exp.
2) call delta increased todaySo their hedging has been successful !?they BOUGHT CALL otm because of positive vanna above 5565?! (Not sure at all)

My answer
1) Same as Leonida, heding the negative extra synthetic delta exposure. I'm not sure it's OTM call or ITM call.
2) I think if price stabilized, and it's ITM call they bought (found two big 0dte itm call on my brokage, so I assume it's JPM's call), it's delta will slightly decrease as time decay. and I think by opening the calls, it make MM increased negative delta exp, so MM will buy back and support the price.

comment.body:
To me, if we are going off of “the common sentiment is the wrong sentiment” indicator, then there seems to be a whole lot of bearish potentiality out there. Seems like the market would be more sensitive to “good news”. Not saying this is the case, as there are alot of things to be bearish about and there is some indication that the market could be “under hedged”. But to say right now we are in a “bull trap” environment at this current level and market sentiment seems like an improper way to characterize this moment.

If we continue further down, just seems to me like we are in a bearish environment and price action would continue to reflect that.

comment.body:
0DTE Road Map – Update (April 01)
Chop Zone:
5560–5610
→ Compression zone if vol stays sticky
→ Vanna convexity supportive if vol drops
IF/THEN Scenarios
Within Chop Zone
IF > 5570 → THEN 5585
IF < 5570 → THEN 5555
If 5550/5570 holds, gamma support active
Expect compression toward 5600 if vol subsides
Above Chop Zone
IF > 5600/5610 → THEN 5625/5630
Watch for compressive drift if charm flows engage
IF > 5635 (vol down) → THEN stabilize above → potential drift
IF > 5650 → THEN test vanna-flip zone → requires vol crush
Below Chop Zone
IF < 5555 → THEN 5535
IF < 5535 → THEN 5525 magnet
IF < 5525 → THEN 5500/5505
Charm + speed flip increases risk of continuation lower
Key Dealer Flow Notes
Zero Vanna Level: 5595
Flat-to-positive → low vol = easier break
Gamma Support: 5550–5570
Positive convexity into afternoon
Only effective if vol compresses
Gamma Flip Zone: 5510
Dealers become longer gamma, but slope is flat → minor vol dampening unless customers push
Charm: Net positive
Supports slow drift upward
Flat negative convexity = gamma decay → bigger moves later if support breaks
#SPX #0DTE #Gamma #Vanna #Charm #Volatility #TradingPlan #MarketFlow #SPXRoadMap
